questions.text: I hold the view that life without intense feeling would not feel like life at all.
I am the person who turns a vague idea into a concrete plan of action.
I believe a project succeeds best with distributed leadership.
I think that the discipline of remaining emotionally composed under pressure is one of the most practical skills a person can have.
I hold the view that soliciting everyone's input is a form of respect.
I believe my purpose is best fulfilled when I am contributing to someone else's well-being.
I find a certain creative energy in disorder that I lose when things are too tidy.
I notice that transitions between emotional states happen gradually for me.
I think of time as a finite resource that should be allocated thoughtfully.
I notice that people sometimes wonder if I'm affected by things, but I simply process them at a lower volume.
I prefer making decisions quickly so I can start learning from the outcomes.
I feel that the moral weight of another's pain creates an imperative that is difficult to override with any competing consideration.
I believe good communication means saying what you mean clearly and directly.
I notice when someone is gauging the room before sharing an opinion.
I routinely choose quality-of-life improvements for myself over gifts for others.
I often do not notice when someone feels excluded from a group.
I believe that the discipline of maintaining a level-headed perspective during crises is what transforms good intentions into effective outcomes.
I feel that personal recognition for my work is important to me.
